# Quote proofs — the folk-quote ledger

<!-- GENERATED by `node scripts/quotecheck.mjs --report` — do not edit by hand. -->

Every `Q()` line in the story — a line presented to the player as *the real*
*thing, quoted from the folk sources* — is registered in [`src/game/quotes.js`](../src/game/quotes.js)
and verified by [`scripts/quotecheck.mjs`](../scripts/quotecheck.mjs). This ledger is the
generated, human-readable form of that register: what the game says, what the
source says, and where to check it.

**34 quoted lines** (33 distinct quotes): 16 corpus · 3 variant · 11 external · 3 oral.

## `kanun-602` — Kanuni i Lekë Dukagjinit, §602

> **shtëpia e shqiptarit është e zotit dhe e mikut**

- **Original:** "Shpija e Shqyptarit asht e Zotit e e mikut."
- **Translation:** The Albanian's house belongs to God and to the guest.
- **Fidelity:** verbatim
- **Proof:** **✓ corpus-verified** — the original is machine-checked against the downloaded primary source below
  - [`docs/references/kanuni-leke-dukagjinit.sq.txt`](https://github.com/miketamis/adventure/blob/main/docs/references/kanuni-leke-dukagjinit.sq.txt#L4154) line 4154: `"Shpija e Shqyptarit asht e Zotit e e mikut"` — Gheg shpija/asht rendered shtëpia/është; the Kanun's "e Zotit e e mikut" smoothed to "e Zotit dhe e mikut".
- **Story node:** `breshka1`

## `kanun-608` — Kanuni i Lekë Dukagjinit, §608

> **bukë e kripë e zemër**

- **Original:** Mikut do t'i bahet nderë: "Bukë e krypë e zêmër".
- **Translation:** The guest shall be honoured: with bread, salt and heart.
- **Fidelity:** verbatim
- **Proof:** **✓ corpus-verified** — the original is machine-checked against the downloaded primary source below
  - [`docs/references/kanuni-leke-dukagjinit.sq.txt`](https://github.com/miketamis/adventure/blob/main/docs/references/kanuni-leke-dukagjinit.sq.txt#L4165) line 4165: `"Bukë e krypë e zerner"` — The OCR of the scan reads zêmër as "zerner"; §609 on the next line repeats the triad legibly ("Buka e krypa e zëmra").
- **Story node:** `plaka`

## `kanun-620` — Kanuni i Lekë Dukagjinit, §620

> **mirë se erdhe**

- **Original:** Po të hini miku në shpi, gjak me të pasë, do t'i thuejsh: "Mirë së erdhe!"
- **Translation:** When the guest enters your house — even if you are in blood with him — you shall say: "Well have you come!"
- **Fidelity:** verbatim
- **Proof:** **✓ corpus-verified** — the original is machine-checked against the downloaded primary source below
  - [`docs/references/kanuni-leke-dukagjinit.sq.txt`](https://github.com/miketamis/adventure/blob/main/docs/references/kanuni-leke-dukagjinit.sq.txt#L4212) line 4212: `"Mirë së erdhe!"`
- **Story node:** `oda1`

## `kanun-631` — Kanuni i Lekë Dukagjinit, §631

> **buka e lan dëmin**

- **Original:** "Buka e lan dâmin."
- **Translation:** The bread washes away the harm. (The reconciliation meal ends the feud.)

## `ymer-welcome` — Ymer Aga — këngë popullore

> **mirë se erdhe, aga ymer**

- **Original:** - Mirë se erdhe Imer Aga!
- **Translation:** Welcome home, Aga Ymer!

## `ymer-besa-kept` — Ymer Aga — këngë popullore

> **mbajte besën që ke dhënë**

- **Original:** majte besën i shqyptarit, / majte besën qi ke dhan-e!
- **Translation:** You kept the besa of the Albanian — you kept the besa you had given!

- **Proof:** **✓ corpus-verified** — the original is machine-checked against the downloaded primary source below

- **Story node:** `agaYmerFund`

## `fishta-invocation` — Lahuta e Malcís — Gjergj Fishta

> **ndihmo, zot, si më ke ndihmuar**

Gheg m'kë ndihmue rendered më ke ndihmuar.
- **Story node:** `kengaLahute`

## `dozon-tale-opener` — formula e përrallës (Dozon, 1879)

> **ish e mos ish**

- **Original:** Iç mos iç ("Il était et il n'était pas")
- **Translation:** There was and there was not — the set opening of the Tosk tales.
- **Fidelity:** verbatim
- **Proof:** **✓ corpus-verified** — the original is machine-checked against the downloaded primary source below
  - [`docs/references/dozon-manuel-langue-chkipe.fr-sq.txt`](https://github.com/miketamis/adventure/blob/main/docs/references/dozon-manuel-langue-chkipe.fr-sq.txt#L1625) line 1625: `Iç mos iç` — Dozon's ç-orthography for sh: "Iç mos iç" = "Ish mos ish". It opens, among others, the very tale the old man in the oda tells (the maiden promised to the Sun: "nœ mbretœréçœ kyœ noûkœ kiç fœmiyœ" — a queen who had no children). Tellers commonly link the pair with e, as the game does.
- **Story nodes:** `agaYmer1`, `odaPlak`

## `dozon-tale-closing` — formula e mbylljes (Dozon, 1879)

> **u mplakën e u trashëguan**

- **Original:** Oumblyâk edhé outraçigoûa. ("U mplak edhe u trashëgua.")
- **Translation:** He grew old and had heirs — the set closing of the Tosk tales.
- **Fidelity:** inflected
- **Proof:** **✓ corpus-verified** — the original is machine-checked against the downloaded primary source below
  - [`docs/references/dozon-manuel-langue-chkipe.fr-sq.txt`](https://github.com/miketamis/adventure/blob/main/docs/references/dozon-manuel-langue-chkipe.fr-sq.txt#L999) line 999: `Oumblyâk edhé outraçigoûa` — Dozon prints the formula in the singular at the end of two tales; the game bends it to the plural (ata u mplakën e u trashëguan) for Aga Ymer and his wife.
- **Story node:** `agaYmerFund`

## `qeni-leh` — fjalë e urtë (Dozon, 1879)

> **qeni që leh nuk kafshon**

- **Original:** Kyéni kyœ lyéh noûkœ kafçón ("Qeni që leh nukë kafshon")
- **Translation:** The dog that barks does not bite. (Dozon's proverb no. 1, from Fier.)
- **Fidelity:** verbatim
- **Proof:** **✓ corpus-verified** — the original is machine-checked against the downloaded primary source below
  - [`docs/references/dozon-manuel-langue-chkipe.fr-sq.txt`](https://github.com/miketamis/adventure/blob/main/docs/references/dozon-manuel-langue-chkipe.fr-sq.txt#L5706) line 5706: `Kyéni kyœ lyéh noûkœ kafçdn` — First entry of Dozon's "Quelques proverbes" supplement; the scan's OCR reads the ó of kafçón as d.
- **Story node:** `qeniGate`

## `me-durim` — fjalë e urtë (Dozon, 1879)

> **me durim bëhen të gjitha**

- **Original:** Me dourim tœ tœra bœkenœ ("Me durim të tëra bëhen")
- **Translation:** With patience all things are done. (Dozon's proverb no. 4.)
- **Fidelity:** inflected

- **Story node:** `mulli1`

## `trashegofshin` — urimi i dasmës

> **u trashëgofshin**

- **Original:** na outraçigofçin ("na u trashëgofshin") — the blessing called at the betrothal and the ring-exchange
- **Translation:** May they have heirs! — the set wedding blessing.
- **Fidelity:** verbatim
- **Proof:** **✓ corpus-verified** — the original is machine-checked against the downloaded primary source below
  - [`docs/references/dozon-manuel-langue-chkipe.fr-sq.txt`](https://github.com/miketamis/adventure/blob/main/docs/references/dozon-manuel-langue-chkipe.fr-sq.txt#L5934) line 5934: `na outraçigofçin` — From Dozon's account of the wedding customs ("VLYÉSA — les fiançailles"): the fathers kiss and wish "na outraçigofçin edhé na oumblyâktçin" — may they have heirs and grow old.
- **Story node:** `dasma1`

## `te-hengte-ujku` — mallkimi i moçëm (von Hahn, përralla 105)

> **të hëngtë ujku**

- **Original:** tœ ngrœntœ oûykou ("të ngrëntë ujku" — Tosk); Gheg: "Të hângtë ujku!"
- **Translation:** May the wolf eat you! — the herdsman's curse the wolf-creation fable explains.
- **Fidelity:** verbatim
- **Proof:** **✓ corpus-verified** — the original is machine-checked against the downloaded primary source below
  - [`docs/references/dozon-manuel-langue-chkipe.fr-sq.txt`](https://github.com/miketamis/adventure/blob/main/docs/references/dozon-manuel-langue-chkipe.fr-sq.txt#L5835) line 5835: `tœ ngrœntœ oûykou, que le loup te` — Dozon's note to proverb no. 37: "allusion à l'imprécation qu'on a coutume d'adresser aux animaux domestiques: tœ ngrœntœ oûykou, que le loup te mange!" The Tosk verb ngrëntë and the game's standard hëngtë are forms of the same optative of ha (to eat).
  - [von Hahn, Griechische und albanesische Märchen, tale 105](https://archive.org/stream/GriechischeUndAlbanesischeMarchenJohannGeorgVonHahn/GriechischeUndAlbanesischeMarchen-JohannGeorgVonHahn_djvu.txt) — The etiological fable ("why the wolf devours") behind the curse, in von Hahn's German translation.
- **Story node:** `ujkuFund`

## `lambertz-closing` — mbyllja e tregimtarit (Lambertz)

> **përralla atje, shëndeti këtej**

- **Original:** Pralla n Lesh, shndetja prei nesh! (Tirana; Lambertz no. 61 — "the tale in the wool, health for us")
- **Translation:** The tale away, the health our way — the teller's sign-off.
- **Fidelity:** adapted
- **Proof:** **≈ formula-verified** — the corpus attests the formula; the exact wording rests on the linked source
  - [`docs/references/lambertz-albanische-marchen.de-sq.txt`](https://github.com/miketamis/adventure/blob/main/docs/references/lambertz-albanische-marchen.de-sq.txt#L17319) line 17319 *(formula variant)*: `Praia n Le§, Snedja prei nes!` — Lambertz no. 61 ("Erklärung zweier Märchenschlüsse", recorded in Tirana) prints the formula — the OCR mangles sh to § and s. The game keeps the formula's shape ("the tale THERE, the health HERE") but simplifies the pun on lesh (wool / the town Lezha) that the original turns on.
- **Story node:** `odaPlak`

## `rozafa-gjiri` — kënga e Rozafës

> **gjirin e djathtë ma lini jashtë**

- **Original:** Gjirin e djathtë ma lini jashtë (the walled mother's plea, as the song and Kuteli's prose keep it)
- **Translation:** Leave my right breast outside the wall (to nurse my son).
- **Fidelity:** verbatim
- **Proof:** **≈ formula-verified** — the corpus attests the formula; the exact wording rests on the linked source
  - [Legjenda e Rozafës — Mitrush Kuteli (full Albanian prose)](https://www.voal.ch/legjenda-e-rozafes-nga-mitrush-kuteli/kulture/letersi/): “Gjirin e djathtë ma lini jashtë”
  - [`docs/references/hecquard-haute-albanie.fr.txt`](https://github.com/miketamis/adventure/blob/main/docs/references/hecquard-haute-albanie.fr.txt#L1629) line 1629 *(formula variant)*: `laissât allaiter` — Hecquard (1858) attests the legend at Shkodër first-hand: the walled woman "demanda qu'on lui laissât allaiter encore une fois son enfant à travers un trou de la muraille" — asked to keep nursing her child through a gap in the wall. A French retelling, so it proves the motif, not the Albanian wording.
- **Story node:** `murosur`

## `doruntina-era` — Kostandini e Doruntina — legjendë

> **pse më vjen erë e dheut**

- **Original:** "Pse më vjen erë dheu, o vëlla?" — Doruntina, riding behind the dead Kostandin
- **Translation:** Why do you smell of earth, brother?
- **Fidelity:** inflected
- **Proof:** **↗ link-verified** — quoted from a printed source we link but do not hold locally
  - [Besa e Kostandinit — the full Albanian ballad](https://www.voal.ch/besa-e-kostandinit-balade-popullore-shqiptare/kulture/letersi/) — The earth-smell exchange on the night ride is the legend's most famous beat; the ballad variants word it slightly differently and the game follows the common prose form.
  - [Constantin and Doruntinë (Wikipedia)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Constantin_and_Doruntin%C3%AB)
- **Story node:** `kostandin3`

## `doruntina-pluhuri` — Kostandini e Doruntina — legjendë

> **është pluhuri i udhës**

- **Original:** "Është pluhuri i udhës" — Kostandin's answer
- **Translation:** It is the dust of the road.
- **Fidelity:** inflected
- **Proof:** **↗ link-verified** — quoted from a printed source we link but do not hold locally
  - [Besa e Kostandinit — the full Albanian ballad](https://www.voal.ch/besa-e-kostandinit-balade-popullore-shqiptare/kulture/letersi/) — The dead brother's deflection, paired with doruntina-era; variants give pluhuri i udhës / pluhuri i rrugës.
- **Story node:** `kostandin3`

## `rona-peperona` — kënga e shiut

> **bjerë shi në arat tona**

- **Original:** Rona, rona, peperona, / bjerë shi ndër arat tona! (the children's dodola rain-chant)
- **Translation:** Rona, rona, butterfly-doll, let rain fall on our fields!
- **Fidelity:** verbatim
- **Proof:** **↗ link-verified** — quoted from a printed source we link but do not hold locally
  - [Dodola / peperona rain rite (Wikipedia)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dodola) — The Albanian chant is recorded in the ethnographic literature (Tirta, Mitologjia ndër shqiptarë) in close variants: "Rona, rona, peperona, bjerë shi ndër arat tona". The game's në for ndër is the only change.
- **Story node:** `dordolec2`

## `te-lumte-krahu` — urimi i trimit

> **të lumtë krahu**

- **Original:** Të lumtë krahu! (the set congratulation on a feat of arms; Fishta: "Të lumtë goja, bajraktar")
- **Translation:** Blessed be your arm!
- **Fidelity:** verbatim
- **Proof:** **≈ formula-verified** — the corpus attests the formula; the exact wording rests on the linked source
  - [`docs/references/fishta-lahuta-e-malcis.sq.txt`](https://github.com/miketamis/adventure/blob/main/docs/references/fishta-lahuta-e-malcis.sq.txt#L9041) line 9041 *(formula variant)*: `Të lumtë goja, bajraktar` — The Lahuta attests the optative formula "të lumtë <limb>" (there with goja, the mouth, praising a speech); krahu (the arm) is its standard martial pair in the living language.
  - *oral:* The exact wording "të lumtë krahu" is the everyday congratulation on a deed of strength; standard usage, lightly attested in the public-domain corpus.
- **Story node:** `shtepia`

## `njeqind-vjec` — urim i moçëm

> **u bëfsh njëqind vjeç**

- **Original:** U bëfsh njëqind vjeç!
- **Translation:** May you live to a hundred! (The optative blessing over a cure or a birthday.)
- **Fidelity:** verbatim
- **Proof:** **~ oral tradition** — a formula of the living language, not located in print (the weakest tier)
  - *oral:* A standard optative blessing of the living language; idiomatically universal but not located in the public-domain corpus.
- **Story node:** `sheruesi`

## `mire-se-ju-gjeta` — përgjigjja e mikut — urim i moçëm

> **mirë se ju gjeta**

- **Original:** Mirë se ju gjeta! — the guest's set reply to "Mirë se erdhe!"
- **Translation:** Well have I found you! (The fixed response to the welcome the Kanun commands.)
- **Fidelity:** verbatim
- **Proof:** **~ oral tradition** — a formula of the living language, not located in print (the weakest tier)
  - *oral:* The universal reply-half of the Albanian greeting pair whose first half the Kanun fixes in §620 (see kanun-620); the pair is standard from the highlands to the south.
- **Story node:** `oda1`

## `bleta-riddle` — gjëegjëzë e moçme

> **punon ditë e natë, na jep dyllë e na jep mjaltë**

- **Original:** Zu-zu-zu, punon ditë e natë, na jep dyllë e na jep mjaltë. (answer: bleta, the bee)
- **Translation:** Buzz-buzz, works day and night, gives us wax and gives us honey.
- **Fidelity:** verbatim
- **Proof:** **~ oral tradition** — a formula of the living language, not located in print (the weakest tier)
  - *oral:* A children's gjëegjëzë from the school-collection tradition; circulates widely in Albanian riddle anthologies but is not located in the public-domain corpus.
- **Story node:** `bletaFund`
